title: Anti-lock Braking System (ABS) — Wheel Slip Control
content: ABS prevents wheel lock-up during emergency braking by modulating hydraulic pressure via solenoid valves, keeping wheel slip ratio λ = (v_vehicle − v_wheel)/v_vehicle between 0.1 and 0.2 where tyre–road friction µ is maximised.
extended_content: ABS cycle: 1) Pressure Hold — solenoid closes inlet, 2) Pressure Release — outlet solenoid opens, 3) Pressure Re-apply — inlet re-opened. Cycle frequency 10–15 Hz. Wheel speed sensors (Hall-effect toothed ring) provide v_wheel at 1 ms resolution. ECU differentiates wheel deceleration: if dω/dt > threshold (typically 20–30 rad/s²) ABS engages. Four-channel ABS controls each wheel independently. Wet road: 30% shorter stopping distance vs locked wheels. ABS does NOT shorten stopping distance on loose gravel (locked may be shorter).
units: λ: dimensionless, v: m/s, ω: rad/s
source: Bosch ABS 8 Technical Manual; ISO 11285:2006 Road vehicles ABS; NHTSA Technical Report DOT HS 811 182
